devanagari: ययातेर्गृहत्यागः । श्रीशुक उवाच । स इत्थं आचरन् कामान् स्त्रैणोऽपह्नवमात्मनः । बुद्ध्वा प्रियायै निर्विण्णो गाथामेतामगायत ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: yayātergṛhatyāgaḥ śrīśuka uvāca sa itthaṃ ācaran kāmān straiṇo buddhvā priyāyai nirviṇṇo gāthāmetāmagāyata

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
(While) Yayāti, the woman-dominated addict, was enjoying the pleasures of senses in this way, he became aware of his spiritual fall (negligence of the Self) and being disenchanted (with worldly pleasures), narrated the following parable to his beloved Devayānī.

devanagari: श्रृणु भार्गव्यमूं गाथां मद्विधाचरितां भुवि । धीरा यस्यानुशोचन्ति वने ग्रामनिवासिनः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrṛṇu bhārgavyamūṃ gāthāṃ madvidhācaritāṃ bhuvi dhīrā yasyānuśocanti vane grāmanivāsinaḥ

translation: “Oh Devayānī! Please listen to this history of a passionate person on this earth, whose life and conduct were just like mine, and whose lot was deplored by self-controlled people residing in forests.

devanagari: बस्त एको वने कश्चिद् विचिन्वन् प्रियमात्मनः । ददर्श कूपे पतितां स्वकर्मवशगामजाम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: basta eko vane kaścid vicinvan priyamātmanaḥ dadarśa kūpe patitāṃ svakarmavaśagāmajām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: A certain goat, while seeking all alone for an object dear to him, in a jungle, happened to see a goat fallen into a well as a result of her fate.

devanagari: तस्या उद्धरणोपायं बस्तः कामी विचिन्तयन् । व्यधत्त तीर्थमुद्धृत्य विषाणाग्रेण रोधसी ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyā uddharaṇopāyaṃ bastaḥ kāmī vicintayan vyadhatta tīrthamuddhṛtya viṣāṇāgreṇa rodhasī

translation: Cherishing lust for her, the goat thought out a way to lift her up and digging out the earth on the bank with the tips of his horns, he made a way out for her.

devanagari: सोत्तीर्य कूपात् सुश्रोणी तमेव चकमे किल । तया वृतं समुद्वीक्ष्य बह्व्योऽजाः कान्तकामिनीः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sottīrya kūpāt suśroṇī tameva cakame kila tayā vṛtaṃ samudvīkṣya bahvyo

translation: It is said that after getting out of the well, the beautiful she-goat loved him (as her husband). Observing that she had selected a stout, finely bearded, lovable he-goat possessing great venereal strength and expert in the art of sexual intercourse, a number of other she-goats desirous of having a mate, wooed him. That he-goat possessed as he was by the devil of sexual passion, excited the passion of that flock of she-goats and revelled with them all alone, and thus failed to realize and think about his own self.

devanagari: पीवानं श्मश्रुलं प्रेष्ठं मीढ्वांसं याभकोविदम् । स एकोऽजवृषस्तासां बह्वीनां रतिवर्धनः । रेमे कामग्रहग्रस्त आत्मानं नावबुध्यत ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: pīvānaṃ śmaśrulaṃ preṣṭhaṃ mīḍhvāṃsaṃ yābhakovidam sa eko reme kāmagrahagrasta ātmānaṃ nāvabudhyata

translation: It is said that after getting out of the well, the beautiful she-goat loved him (as her husband). Observing that she had selected a stout, finely bearded, lovable he-goat possessing great venereal strength and expert in the art of sexual intercourse, a number of other she-goats desirous of having a mate, wooed him. That he-goat possessed as he was by the devil of sexual passion, excited the passion of that flock of she-goats and revelled with them all alone, and thus failed to realize and think about his own self.

devanagari: तमेव प्रेष्ठतमया रममाणमजान्यया । विलोक्य कूपसंविग्ना नामृष्यद् बस्तकर्म तत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tameva preṣṭhatamayā ramamāṇamajānyayā vilokya kūpasaṃvignā nāmṛṣyad bastakarma tat

translation: Seeing him enjoying himself with another most beautiful she-goat, the female-goat that formerly suffered a fall in the well, did not tolerate that act of the he-goat.

devanagari: तं दुर्हृदं सुहृद्रूपं कामिनं क्षणसौहृदम् । इन्द्रियारामं उत्सृज्य स्वामिनं दुःखिता ययौ ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ durhṛdaṃ suhṛdrūpaṃ kāminaṃ kṣaṇasauhṛdam indriyārāmaṃ utsṛjya svāminaṃ duḥkhitā yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Being distressed, she left her mate who was wicked- hearted, a false lover posing as a friend, and professing love only for a moment, but really given to the gratification of his senses, and went to her master.

devanagari: सोऽपि चानुगतः स्त्रैणः कृपणस्तां प्रसादितुम् । कुर्वन्निडविडाकारं नाशक्नोत् पथि संधितुम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: so kurvanniḍaviḍākāraṃ nāśaknot pathi saṃdhitum

translation: Even that goat too, being a woman-addict, felt aggrieved and followed her to reconcile hen but in spite of his bleating with erotic eloquence he failed to win her over on the way.

devanagari: तस्य तत्र द्विजः कश्चित् अजास्वाम्यच्छिनद् रुषा । लम्बन्तं वृषणं भूयः सन्दधेऽर्थाय योगवित् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tasya tatra dvijaḥ kaścit ajāsvāmyacchinad ruṣā lambantaṃ vṛṣaṇaṃ bhūyaḥ sandadhe

translation: There, her master, a certain Brāhmaṇa, castrated the dangling scrotum of that goat. But in the interest (of the she-goat), he, being a master of (such) expedients sewed back the amputated limb.

devanagari: संबद्धवृषणः सोऽपि ह्यजया कूपलब्धया । कालं बहुतिथं भद्रे कामैर्नाद्यापि तुष्यति ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃbaddhavṛṣaṇaḥ so kālaṃ bahutithaṃ bhadre kāmairnādyāpi tuṣyati

translation: Having got his testicles (along with his capacity of sexual enjoyment) restored, the goat, though enjoyed pleasures for a long time with the she-goat found in the well, does not feel satiated with sexual pleasures even today.

devanagari: तथाहं कृपणः सुभ्रु भवत्याः प्रेमयन्त्रितः । आत्मानं नाभिजानामि मोहितस्तव मायया ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāhaṃ kṛpaṇaḥ subhru bhavatyāḥ premayantritaḥ ātmānaṃ nābhijānāmi mohitastava māyayā

translation: In the same way, Oh lady with beautiful eyebrows, I, a person of poor intellect, am chained down by your love, Oh gracious lady. Being infatuated by your charms, I do not know (the nature of) my Self.

devanagari: यत् पृथिव्यां व्रीहियवं हिरण्यं पशवः स्त्रियः । न दुह्यन्ति मनःप्रीतिं पुंसः कामहतस्य ते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yat pṛthivyāṃ vrīhiyavaṃ hiraṇyaṃ paśavaḥ striyaḥ na duhyanti manaḥprītiṃ puṃsaḥ kāmahatasya te

translation: All the food-grains, all gold, animals and women cannot yield satisfaction (to the degree of satiation) to the mind of a person subjected to cravings and passions.

devanagari: न जातु कामः कामानां उपभोगेन शाम्यति । हविषा कृष्णवर्त्मेव भूय एवाभिवर्धते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānāṃ upabhogena śāmyati haviṣā kṛṣṇavartmeva bhūya evābhivardhate
translation: Never, never does passion get satiated with the enjoyment of its sense-objects. (On the contrary) like a fire fed with oblations of ghee, it grows again in intensity.

devanagari: यदा न कुरुते भावं सर्वभूतेष्वमंगलम् । समदृष्टेस्तदा पुंसः सर्वाः सुखमया दिशः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā na kurute bhāvaṃ sarvabhūteṣvamaṃgalam samadṛṣṭestadā puṃsaḥ sarvāḥ sukhamayā diśaḥ
translation: When one does not cherish inauspicious attitudes (e.g. of hatred, partiality) to living beings, and looks upon all with equality, all the directions are full of bliss to him (i.e. he is blessed with happiness from all quarters).

devanagari: या दुस्त्यजा दुर्मतिभिः जीर्यतो या न जीर्यते । तां तृष्णां दुःखनिवहां शर्मकामो द्रुतं त्यजेत् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yā dustyajā durmatibhiḥ jīryato yā na jīryate tāṃ tṛṣṇāṃ duḥkhanivahāṃ śarmakāmo drutaṃ tyajet

translation: A person desirous of happiness (untarnished by misery), should instantly give up thirst (for happiness) which is difficult to get rid of by evil-minded persons, and which does not get worn out even if one’s body gets withered with age, and which is a source of endless misery.

devanagari: मात्रा स्वस्रा दुहित्रा वा नाविविक्तासनो भवेत् । बलवान् इन्द्रियग्रामो विद्वांसमपि कर्षति ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: mātrā svasrā duhitrā vā nāviviktāsano bhavet balavān indriyagrāmo vidvāṃsamapi karṣati

translation: A person should not sit very close in privacy (lit. share the same seat in private) to his mother, sister or daughter. For the senses (as a whole) are so powerful that they lead astray even a wise (learned) man.

devanagari: पूर्णं वर्षसहस्रं मे विषयान् सेवतोऽसकृत् । तथापि चानुसवनं तृष्णा तेषूपजायते ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrṇaṃ varṣasahasraṃ me viṣayān sevato tathāpi cānusavanaṃ tṛṣṇā teṣūpajāyate

translation: It is for complete one thousand years that I have been enjoying repeatedly and incessantly the objects of pleasures. Still every time (I enjoy them), the hankering after them grows (apace).

devanagari: तस्मादेतामहं त्यक्त्वा ब्रह्मण्यध्याय मानसम् । निर्द्वन्द्वो निरहंकारः चरिष्यामि मृगैः सह ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādetāmahaṃ tyaktvā brahmaṇyadhyāya mānasam nirdvandvo nirahaṃkāraḥ cariṣyāmi mṛgaiḥ saha
translation: Therefore giving up this (thirst for enjoyment) and concentrating my mind on the Brahman and freeing myself from the pairs of opposites (pleasure-pain etc.) and the notion of egotism (I and mine), I shall lead a life in forest.

devanagari: दृष्टं श्रुतमसद् बुद्ध्वा नानुध्यायेन्न सन्दिशेत् । संसृतिं चात्मनाशं च तत्र विद्वान् स आत्मदृक् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭaṃ śrutamasad buddhvā nānudhyāyenna sandiśet saṃsṛtiṃ cātmanāśaṃ ca tatra vidvān sa ātmadṛk

[nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation: Knowing the unreality (momentariness) of what is seen (the kingdom or pleasures in this world) and what is heard (from the Vedas about the pleasures and positions promised in the next world), one should not brood over them or enjoy them. For, in that lies the transmigration of the soul, and the loss of (the real knowledge of) the soul. Such a knower realizes the Atman”.

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा नाहुषो जायां तदीयं पूरवे वयः । दत्त्वा स्वां जरसं तस्माद् आददे विगतस्पृहः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā nāhuṣo jāyāṃ tadīyaṃ pūrave vayaḥ dattvā svāṃ jarasaṃ tasmād ādade vigataspṛhaḥ
translation: Having addressed thus to his wife (Devayānī), Yayāti, the son of Nahuṣa, returned his youth to Pūru, and becoming free from desires, accepted the decrepitude of his old age from him.

devanagari: दिशि दक्षिणपूर्वस्यां द्रुह्युं दक्षिणतो यदुम् । प्रतीच्यां तुर्वसुं चक्र उदीच्यामनुमीश्वरम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: diśi dakṣiṇapūrvasyāṃ druhyuṃ dakṣiṇato yadum pratīcyāṃ turvasuṃ cakra udīcyāmanumīśvaram

translation: He appointed Druhyu to rule over the Southeastern region (of his kingdom), Yadu in the South, Turvasu in the west and Anu in the north.

devanagari: भूमण्डलस्य सर्वस्य पूरुमर्हत्तमं विशाम् । अभिषिच्याग्रजान् तस्य वशे स्थाप्य वनं ययौ ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūmaṇḍalasya sarvasya pūrumarhattamaṃ viśām abhiṣicyāgrajān tasya vaśe sthāpya vanaṃ yayau

translation: Having consecrated Pūru, the worthiest of them all, as the sovereign ruler of the earth and the subjects, he placed the elder brothers under his control and then repaired to the forest.

devanagari: आसेवितं वर्षपूगान् षड्वर्गं विषयेषु सः । क्षणेन मुमुचे नीडं जातपक्ष इव द्विजः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: āsevitaṃ varṣapūgān ṣaḍvargaṃ viṣayeṣu saḥ kṣaṇena mumuce nīḍaṃ jātapakṣa iva dvijaḥ

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Just as a bird leaves its nest as soon as it gets full- fledged, he instantly shed off the six-fold objects of pleasures enjoyed through his senses and mind, for a considerable number of years (or) controlled his senses and mind.

devanagari: स तत्र निर्मुक्तसमस्तसंग । आत्मानुभूत्या विधुतत्रिलिंगः । परेऽमले ब्रह्मणि वासुदेवे । लेभे गतिं भागवतीं प्रतीतः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tatra nirmuktasamastasaṃga ātmānubhūtyā vidhutatriliṃgaḥ pare lebhe gatiṃ bhāgavatīṃ pratītaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: [1] Freeing himself completely from all attachments and shedding off his subtle body constituted of three guṇas by virtue of his spiritual power of self realization, the celebrated king Yayāti became absorbed[2] in the Supreme Brahman unsoiled by Māyā (or any impurity) and designated as Vāsudeva—a state attained by the devotees of the Supreme Lord.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī By his direct perception of the Self, he shed off his egotism caused by three guṇas or the threefold misery caused by them and became liberated—a stage reached by following the Bhāgavata dharma.

Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā He approached the stage of Mokṣa, for according to the Mahābhārata, he spent some period in the celestial world.

devanagari: श्रुत्वा गाथां देवयानी मेने प्रस्तोभमात्मनः । स्त्रीपुंसोः स्नेहवैक्लव्यात् परिहासमिवेरितम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā gāthāṃ devayānī mene prastobhamātmanaḥ strīpuṃsoḥ snehavaiklavyāt parihāsamiveritam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Hearing that parable, Devayānī regarded it as an incentive to follow a path of renunciation, and a jocular story told out of agony of separation between a man and his wife.

devanagari: सा सन्निवासं सुहृदां प्रपायामिव गच्छताम् । विज्ञायेश्वर तन्त्राणां मायाविरचितं प्रभोः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: sā sannivāsaṃ suhṛdāṃ prapāyāmiva gacchatām vijñāyeśvara tantrāṇāṃ māyāviracitaṃ prabhoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ rac class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ rac class 10 verb]

translation: Realizing that the association of friends and relatives is brought about by Māyā (according to their karma)[2] and is controlled by the Almighty Lord, and thus is as temporary as those of travellers attending a watering place (to drink water on the way), Devayānī, the scion of the Bhṛgu race, gave up attachment to everything and everywhere regarding it as unreal as dreams. She concentrated her mind on Kṛṣṇa (i. e. Lord Viṣṇu) and shuffled off her (subtle) body (and became liberated).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā He approached the stage of Mokṣa, for according to the Mahābhārata, he spent some period in the celestial world.

devanagari: सर्वत्र संगघ्गमुत्सृज्य स्वप्नौपम्येन भार्गवी । कृष्णे मनः समावेश्य व्यधुनोल्लिंगमात्मनः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvatra saṃgaghgamutsṛjya svapnaupamyena bhārgavī kṛṣṇe manaḥ samāveśya vyadhunolliṃgamātmanaḥ
translation: Realizing that the association of friends and relatives is brought about by Māyā (according to their karma)[2] and is controlled by the Almighty Lord, and thus is as temporary as those of travellers attending a watering place (to drink water on the way), Devayānī, the scion of the Bhṛgu race, gave up attachment to everything and everywhere regarding it as unreal as dreams. She concentrated her mind on Kṛṣṇa (i. e. Lord Viṣṇu) and shuffled off her (subtle) body (and became liberated).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā He approached the stage of Mokṣa, for according to the Mahābhārata, he spent some period in the celestial world.

devanagari: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते वासुदेवाय वेधसे । सर्वभूताधिवासाय शान्ताय बृहते नमः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: namastubhyaṃ bhagavate vāsudevāya vedhase sarvabhūtādhivāsāya śāntāya bṛhate namaḥ

[dative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [dative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation:
I bow to you the glorious Lord Vāsudeva, the cause of the Universe, the abode of all beings (or the Inner Controller of all) perfectly serene and all-pervading. Salutations to you.
